text.title: Value Stream Management — Agentic Workflow and Subagents
text.summary: Flow-instrumenter and bottleneck-analyzer pipeline, prompt patterns, and AI-specific failure modes in VSM automation.

section.title: Agentic VSM Pipeline

rule.statement: A flow-instrumenter subagent maps the actual value stream by tracing IDs across tools (issue → branch → PR → deploy → incident → support ticket), normalizes timestamps into stream stages, and emits per-stage metrics.
rule.rationale: Manual cross-tool ID linking is error-prone and stale within a week; automated tracing from authoritative event logs is the only way to maintain a live VSM.

section.p: A DORA collector runs daily on git + deploy logs. A bottleneck-analyzer applies Theory of Constraints weekly, flags the constraint stage, and proposes 1-3 experiments. Humans choose which experiment to run; agents track its effect.
section.reference: Schema for a single traced work item across VSM stages
Bash script to compute last-30-day DORA from git + deploy log

list.item: flow-instrumenter — wires Jira/Linear, GitHub, deploy logs, Sentry; traces item IDs cross-tool; produces stream JSON per work item.
dora-collector — daily DORA: deploy frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, MTTR from git + PagerDuty.
bottleneck-analyzer — applies Little's Law; identifies constraint by highest (cycle time × WIP); requires at least 50 items per work type before declaring a bottleneck.
experiment-tracker — A/B's a process change (e.g., trunk-based vs. feature-branch) on flow metrics; tracks before/after lead time distribution.

example.note: Flow-instrumenter prompt — skips items with missing stages rather than hallucinating timestamps:
example.code: You are a flow-instrumenter. Trace work item {issue_id} across tools using mapping {tool_id_links.yaml}. Emit JSON {item_id, stages: [{name, enter_ts, exit_ts, blocked_h, type}], outcome: shipped|cancelled, work_type: feature| defect|risk|debt}. Skip items with > 1 missing stage — do not interpolate. All timestamps in UTC ISO 8601.

example.note: Bottleneck-analyzer prompt — requires minimum sample before declaring constraint:
example.code: You are a bottleneck-analyzer. Given last 90 days of flow data {flow.json}, return: (1) constraint stage by Little's Law (highest cycle time x WIP), (2) flow distribution across work types, (3) top 3 experiments to elevate the constraint, ranked by expected throughput lift / cost. Require >= 50 items per work type before declaring a bottleneck. Flag insufficient data explicitly rather than guessing.

antipattern.description: Agent hallucinates ID links across tools using loose regex matching.
antipattern.reason: ID linking is the failure point; agents using fuzzy matching will connect unrelated items, producing fabricated flow metrics. Require explicit referenced-by chains only.

antipattern.description: Timezone bugs in lead-time calculation produce negative or inflated durations.
antipattern.reason: GitLab, GitHub, Jira, and PagerDuty return timestamps in different timezone representations; force UTC normalization before any arithmetic.

antipattern.description: AI-generated PRs inflate Deployment Frequency without raising Earned Value.
antipattern.reason: Bot PRs skew throughput metrics; tag bot commits and report human/bot split separately to prevent gaming of DORA scores.

antipattern.description: Constraint declared after 5 items in a work type.
antipattern.reason: Small samples produce statistically meaningless bottleneck declarations that flip month-to-month. Require at least 50 items per work type before applying Little's Law.

list.item: Webhook-driven instrumentation drops events under load; reconcile with periodic full pulls.
The 5th DORA metric (Reliability) is org-defined — never let an agent pick the SLO target unilaterally.
Mandatory human checkpoints: experiment selection, SLO changes, constraint declaration that triggers org-level process changes.
